title: Dave Inc. Q1 2026 Earnings: Record Growth and Raised Full-Year Guidance
text:
Dave Inc. exceeded expectations in Q1 2026 with strong revenue growth. The company reported revenue of $158.4 million, representing a 47% year-over-year increase and surpassing analyst forecasts of $151.83 million.

Key Financial Metrics:
- Adjusted EBITDA grew 57% to $69.3 million with a 44% margin
- Adjusted diluted EPS reached $3.64, beating estimates of $2.67 by 36.33%
- GAAP net income increased 101% to $57.9 million
- Monthly Transacting Members (MTMs) grew 18% to 2.99 million
- Average Revenue Per User (ARPU) expanded 24% year-over-year

Growth Drivers: The company demonstrated durability in its growth algorithm. CEO Jason Wilk noted that revenue grew 47% year-over-year while the company maintained strong credit metrics. The 28-day past due rate improved to 1.69%, marking the lowest Q1 performance on record. Member acquisition remained efficient with a customer acquisition cost of $18, flat year-over-year. ExtraCash originations reached $2.1 billion, up 37% year-over-year.

Strategic Initiatives: Dave Flex, the new pay-in-four credit product, launched during the quarter. This offering uses CashAI technology for underwriting and aims to provide a responsible alternative to traditional credit cards with no compound interest, no late fees and flexible paycheck-aligned payments. The company also removed its $15 fee cap for new members and introduced Second Draw, allowing members to access unused credit limits within the same pay period.

Forward Guidance:
- Revenue: $710-$720 million (28-30% growth)
- Adjusted EBITDA: $305-$315 million
- Adjusted diluted EPS: $16.25-$16.75
- Expected EPS for Q2 2026: $3.96 and for Q3 2026: $4.44

Market Response: Following the earnings announcement, Dave's stock price rose 0.48% in aftermarket trading to $280.50, despite a 5.5% decline during regular trading hours.
